 {"id":"beads_rust-5ui7","title":"E2E tests: completions command","description":"# E2E Tests for `completions` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br completions bash` - Generate bash completions
- `br completions zsh` - Generate zsh completions
- `br completions fish` - Generate fish completions
- `br completions powershell` - Generate PowerShell completions

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. Generate bash completions → valid bash script
2. Generate zsh completions → valid zsh script
3. Generate fish completions → valid fish script
4. Generate PowerShell completions → valid ps1

### Validation
5. Bash completions contain subcommand names
6. Completions contain flag names
7. Output is parseable by target shell (syntax check)

### Edge Cases
8. Unknown shell type → error or list options
9. Output to file vs stdout
10. Idempotent (same output each run)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_completions.rs
- [ ] 10+ test functions
- [ ] Verify completions are syntactically valid","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SapphireDesert","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:27:37.966583650Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T17:06:10.328544159Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T17:06:10.328544159Z","close_reason":"Implemented 16 E2E tests for completions command covering all 5 shell types (bash, zsh, fish, powershell, elvish), subcommand/flag verification, and edge cases","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-5ui7","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"beads_rust-5wh0","title":"[MEDIUM] Dual OutputContext types: format::OutputContext is shadowed dead code","description":"Two distinct OutputContext/OutputMode types exist in the public API:

1. src/format/context.rs — OutputContext with 4 modes (Rich, Plain, Json, Quiet)
2. src/output/context.rs — OutputContext with 5 modes (Rich, Plain, Json, Toon, Quiet)

All 37 CLI commands import from crate::output::OutputContext. The format::OutputContext is only consumed by format::syntax::highlight_code() and format::markdown::render_markdown(), which are public but never called from any command or output code.

Impact:
- Library consumers constructing format::OutputContext get the wrong type for highlight_code/render_markdown
- Toon output mode is silently unavailable through the format API
- Name collision makes crate documentation confusing

Fix: Either deprecate+remove format::context.rs (breaking public API) or migrate syntax.rs and markdown.rs to accept output::OutputContext.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:00:32.363744391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:00:32.363744391Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","debt"]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-5xp","title":"Feature: CLI Skeleton with Clap Derive","description":"# CLI Skeleton (clap derive)

## Purpose
Define the CLI surface and global flags to match classic bd semantics (non-invasive). This bead sets the **command list**, **global flags**, and output mode expectations.

## Global Flags (classic)
- `--db <path>`: DB path (auto-discover .beads/*.db if not set)
- `--actor <name>`: actor for audit trail
- `--json`: JSON output
- `--no-daemon`: force direct mode (daemon excluded in br)
- `--no-auto-flush`: skip auto JSONL export
- `--no-auto-import`: skip auto import (error if stale)
- `--allow-stale`: bypass freshness check with warning
- `--lock-timeout <ms|duration>`: SQLite busy timeout
- `--no-db`: JSONL-only mode
- `--verbose/-v`, `--quiet/-q`

## Command Set (classic v1)
Required:
- `init`, `create`, `update`, `close`, `reopen`, `delete`
- `list`, `show`, `ready`, `blocked`, `search`
- `dep`, `label`, `comments`
- `stats`/`status`, `count`, `stale`, `orphans`
- `defer`, `undefer`
- `sync --flush-only`, `sync --import-only`
- `config`
Optional (post-core): `where`, `info`, `version`, `q`, `lint`, `graph`, `epic`.

## Output Modes
- Text (default), JSON (`--json`).
- `--json` output must be stable; errors are **inconsistent** in legacy:
  - Some fatal errors emit `{ "error": "..." }` to **stdout**.
  - Others emit JSON error to **stderr**.
  - Some paths still emit plain text even in JSON mode.
We must document + test these legacy quirks for parity.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Help output shows correct commands + global flags.
- JSON mode routing is consistent with bd’s legacy behavior.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:14:08.713303584Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:46:47.844086688Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:46:47.844086688Z","close_reason":"Implemented CLI skeleton with clap in src/cli/mod.rs and src/main.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-5y9e","title":"E2E scenarios: error handling + exit code parity","description":"E2E tests that validate error paths and exit codes across br (and conformance vs bd where applicable).

Scope
- Invalid ID/status/type/priority; missing workspace; ambiguous IDs; dependency cycles.
- Sync error codes (conflict markers, path traversal, prefix mismatch).
- Verify StructuredError JSON output shape and correct exit codes.

Acceptance
- For each category, tests assert exit code + JSON error payload matches docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md.
- Conformance mode compares bd/br error behavior where bd supports the same error path.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:53:01.586255434Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T04:40:41.473459080Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T04:40:41.473459080Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive E2E error tests: invalid type, invalid priority, --no-color mode, text vs JSON parity, exit code categories.  {"id":"beads_rust-axr4","title":"E2E tests: history command","description":"# E2E Tests for `history` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br history list` - List backup snapshots
- `br history save` - Create backup
- `br history restore <id>` - Restore from backup
- `br history --json` - JSON output

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. Save creates backup, verify in list
2. Save multiple backups, verify chronological order
3. Restore backup, verify data restored
4. JSON output structure

### Error Cases
5. History before init → error
6. Restore non-existent backup → error
7. Restore with uncommitted changes → warning

### Edge Cases
8. Backup with 1000+ issues
9. Backup after sync
10. Restore to different branch (git context)
11. Backup retention/cleanup

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_history.rs
- [ ] 11+ test functions
- [ ] Verify backup files in .beads/.br_history/","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OpusClaude","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:27:04.155203750Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T17:36:22.923571913Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T17:36:22.923571913Z","close_reason":"Completed E2E tests for history command: 15 tests covering list, diff, restore, prune, error cases, and edge cases. Also fixed proptest_hash.rs to match updated content_hash_from_parts signature.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-axr4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

All E2E workflow gaps now addressed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T16:18:47.223827825Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T04:45:49.216437701Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T04:45:49.216437701Z","close_reason":"E2E tests for stats, config, and reopen commands added to tests/e2e_queries.rs. All tests pass. All workflow gaps addressed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
+{"id":"beads_rust-b2da","title":"[LOW] BeadsError::Other (anyhow bridge) still used in 7 production sites","description":"The BeadsError::Other(anyhow::Error) variant was added as a 'gradual migration bridge' from anyhow to structured errors. Seven production sites still use it:

1. src/cli/commands/upgrade.rs:219 — self-update error wrapping
2. src/cli/commands/version.rs:290,299,304,310 — curl/GitHub API errors (4 sites)
3. src/storage/sqlite.rs:2107 — consistency assertion (id mismatch)
4. src/sync/mod.rs:2397,2407 — post-import FK validation (2 sites)
5. src/error/context.rs:66 — OptionExt trait implementation

Additionally, logging.rs uses anyhow::Result directly instead of BeadsError::Result.

All Other errors map to ErrorCode::InternalError (exit code 1) with zero structured context — agents get no hint, no retryability signal, and no context field.

Fix: Replace with specific BeadsError variants or use existing structured variants (e.g., Database for sqlite.rs, Config for version.rs).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:00:58.117601418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:00:58.117601418Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["debt","error-handling"]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-b4yu","title":"Implement --robot-help flag for machine-readable help","description":"# --robot-help Flag Implementation

## Purpose
Provide machine-readable help output that AI coding agents can parse to understand available commands, flags, and their semantics.

## Technical Requirements

### Output Format
```json
{
  "name": "br",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Rust port of beads issue tracker",
  "commands": [
    {
      "name": "create",
      "description": "Create a new issue",
      "aliases": ["new", "add"],
      "flags": [
        {
          "name": "--title",
          "short": "-t",
          "type": "string",
          "required": true,
          "description": "Issue title"
        },
        {
          "name": "--priority",
          "short": "-p",
          "type": "integer",
          "required": false,
          "default": 2,
          "valid_range": [0, 4],
          "description": "Priority level (0=critical, 4=backlog)"
        }
      ],
      "examples": [
        "br create --title 'Fix login bug' --priority 1"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "global_flags": [
    {
      "name": "--json",
      "description": "Output in JSON format"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Implementation
- Add `--robot-help` flag to main CLI
- Generate JSON from clap's command structure
- Include all subcommands recursively
- Include examples for each command
- Include valid value ranges/enums

### Clap Integration
```rust
fn generate_robot_help(cmd: \u0026Command) -> serde_json::Value {
    // Recursively build JSON from clap Command
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `br --robot-help` outputs valid JSON
- [ ] All commands are documented
- [ ] All flags include types and constraints
- [ ] Examples are included
- [ ] Output is deterministic (sorted keys)

## References
- cass --robot-help implementation
- clap Command introspection API","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T18:49:19.430131440Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:54:36.656781569Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T18:54:36.656781569Z","close_reason":"ERROR: --robot-help is bv's domain. bv provides all robot-mode flags.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-gpq","title":"Create README.md with quick-start guide","description":"# README.md Creation

## Purpose
Create a comprehensive README.md that serves as the project landing page, enabling users to understand and start using br within 30 seconds.

## Structure

### Header Section
```markdown
# br - Beads Rust 🦀

A fast, non-invasive issue tracker for git repositories. Rust port of [beads](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads).

[\![CI](https://github.com/.../actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](...)
[\![Crates.io](https://img.shields.io/crates/v/beads-rust.svg)](...)
[\![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](...)
```

### Quick Install
```markdown
## Quick Install

\`\`\`bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh | bash
\`\`\`

Or with Homebrew:
\`\`\`bash
brew install dicklesworthstone/tap/br
\`\`\`
```

### TL;DR
```markdown
## TL;DR

br is a local-first issue tracker that stores issues in SQLite with JSONL export for git-based collaboration. It's designed to be non-invasive: no daemons, no git hooks, no auto-commits.

\`\`\`bash
br init                              # Initialize in current repo
br create "Fix login bug" -p 1       # Create high-priority bug
br list                              # Show all issues
br ready                             # Show actionable work
br close bd-abc123                   # Close an issue
br sync --flush-only                 # Export to JSONL for git
\`\`\`
```

### Features Table
```markdown
## Features

| Feature | Status | Description |
|---------|--------|-------------|
| Issue CRUD | ✅ | Create, read, update, delete issues |
| Dependencies | ✅ | Block/unblock relationships |
| Labels | ✅ | Categorize with custom labels |
| Search | ✅ | Full-text search across issues |
| JSONL Sync | ✅ | Git-friendly export/import |
| AI Agent Mode | 🚧 | Structured output for AI tools |
| TUI Mode | 📋 | Interactive terminal UI |
```

### AI Agent Integration
```markdown
## AI Agent Integration

br is designed to work seamlessly with AI coding agents like Claude Code:

\`\`\`bash
# Get machine-readable help
br --robot-help

# Get prioritized work for agent
br --robot-triage

# Structured JSON output
br list --json
\`\`\`

See [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) for complete agent integration guide.
```

### Quick Example
```markdown
## Quick Example

\`\`\`bash
# Initialize br in your project
cd my-project
br init

# Create your first issue
br create --title "Implement user authentication" --type feature --priority 1

# Add a dependency
br create --title "Set up database schema" --type task
br dep add bd-xyz bd-abc  # xyz depends on abc

# See what's ready to work on
br ready

# Claim work
br update bd-abc --status in_progress

# Complete and sync
br close bd-abc --reason "Schema implemented"
br sync --flush-only
git add .beads/ && git commit -m "Update issues"
\`\`\`
```

### Architecture
```markdown
## Architecture

\`\`\`
┌─────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
│   CLI (br)  │────▶│ SQLite Store │────▶│ JSONL Sync  │
└─────────────┘     └──────────────┘     └─────────────┘
                           │                    │
                           ▼                    ▼
                    .beads/beads.db      .beads/issues.jsonl
\`\`\`

- **SQLite**: Primary storage, WAL mode, concurrent access
- **JSONL**: Git-friendly export for collaboration
- **No daemon**: Simple CLI, no background processes
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] README.md exists at project root
- [ ] Installation instructions are correct
- [ ] Quick example works as written
- [ ] All links are valid
- [ ] Badges display correctly
- [ ] Mobile-friendly (readable on GitHub mobile)

## Dependencies
- Installation script (for accurate install commands)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenPuma","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T18:51:07.257879900Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T22:54:31.424817281Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T22:54:31.424817281Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive README.md with quick-start guide, features table, AI agent integration, architecture diagram, safety model reference, and full command reference. Fixed LICENSE link. All other links verified.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-gs0","title":"Phase 5: Polish & Conformance - Production Readiness","description":"# Phase 5: Polish & Conformance

## Goals
Deliver a production-ready `br` with **classic bd parity**, comprehensive testing, and ergonomic utility commands that do not expand scope beyond the three planning docs.

## Deliverables (children)
### Utility/Inspection Commands
- `doctor` (read-only diagnostics; no auto-fixes)
- `info`, `where`, `version`
- `stats/status`, `count`, `stale`, `orphans`
- `defer` / `undefer`
- `epic status` + `close-eligible`
- `graph` (deps visualization)
- `audit` (interactions.jsonl)
- `history` (.br_history) + `changelog`
- `q` (quick capture)
- `lint` (template/config validation)
- `saved queries` (save/run/list/delete)
- CSV export for list/export

### Test & QA Infrastructure
- Unit test infra and specs
- Snapshot/golden tests for human output
- E2E integration tests (CLI workflows)
- Conformance harness (bd vs br JSON parity)
- CI pipeline (fmt + clippy + tests)

### UX & Performance Polish
- Color output (opt-in or auto)
- Progress indicators for long ops
- Shell completions
- Performance benchmarks (startup + common paths)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Conformance suite green; JSON parity with bd for classic commands.
- Utility commands are stable, documented, and tested.
- CI runs `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, and all tests.
- Performance baselines recorded; regressions detectable.

## Notes
- No daemon, no git hooks, no auto-git behavior.
- Utility commands must be read-only unless explicitly documented.

+{"id":"beads_rust-gs85","title":"[LOW] BeadsError::Config misused as catch-all for non-config errors","description":"BeadsError::Config(String) is used for 20+ error sites that are NOT configuration errors:

- changelog.rs (12 sites): git command failures ('Failed to run git'), git reference resolution, date parsing
- orphans.rs (3 sites): IO errors, regex parse failures
- close.rs, reopen.rs, defer.rs, label.rs: internal consistency errors ('produced mismatched counts')
- show.rs: routing/resolution errors

All of these get ErrorCode::ConfigError and exit code 7, but they should be:
- Exit 8 (IoError) for command execution and IO failures
- Exit 1 (InternalError) for consistency violations
- Exit 6 (sync-related) for some resolution errors

Impact: AI agents parsing structured error output get wrong error category signals, which affects retry/recovery logic.

Reproducer: br changelog --since=nonexistent-tag → exits with code 7 (config) instead of 8 (IO) or a git-specific code.

Fix: Add BeadsError variants for external command failure and internal assertion, or use existing Io/Other variants where appropriate.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:00:47.541816460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:00:47.541816460Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","error-handling"]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-gu7b","title":"Code coverage reporting with tarpaulin","description":"# Code Coverage Reporting

## Overview
Configure cargo-tarpaulin for coverage reporting and CI integration.

## Installation
```bash
cargo install cargo-tarpaulin
```

## Configuration

### tarpaulin.toml
```toml
[coverage]
ignore-tests = true
follow-exec = true
timeout = "300s"
out = ["Html", "Lcov", "Json"]
output-dir = "coverage"

[coverage.run]
jobs = 4

[coverage.exclude]
# Exclude benchmarks and examples
paths = ["benches/*", "examples/*"]

# Exclude test helpers
test-patterns = ["test_helpers::*"]
```

## Coverage Targets

### Module Coverage Goals
| Module | Target | Priority |
|--------|--------|----------|
| src/storage/ | 90%+ | Critical - core data layer |
| src/sync/ | 85%+ | Critical - data integrity |
| src/cli/commands/ | 80%+ | High - user-facing |
| src/model/ | 85%+ | High - domain logic |
| src/format/ | 75%+ | Medium - output formatting |
| src/util/ | 70%+ | Medium - helpers |

### Overall Goals
- Minimum threshold: 70%
- Target: 85%+
- Stretch: 90%+

## Logging Requirements

### Coverage Run Logging
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# coverage.sh - Run with logging

echo "coverage_run_start: $(date -Iseconds)"
echo "coverage_config: ignore_tests=true output_dir=coverage"

# Run tarpaulin with verbose output
cargo tarpaulin --out Html --out Lcov --out Json 2>&1 | tee coverage/tarpaulin.log

# Parse and log results
COVERAGE=$(jq '.coverage_percent' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)
LINES_COVERED=$(jq '.covered_lines' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)
TOTAL_LINES=$(jq '.total_lines' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)

echo "coverage_result: percent=${COVERAGE} covered=${LINES_COVERED} total=${TOTAL_LINES}"

# Per-module breakdown
echo "coverage_module_breakdown:"
jq -r '.files[] | "  \(.path): \(.coverage)%"' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json

echo "coverage_run_end: $(date -Iseconds)"
```

### CI Integration Logging
```yaml
# .github/workflows/coverage.yml
- name: Generate coverage
  run: |
    echo "::group::Coverage Generation"
    cargo tarpaulin --out Lcov --out Json 2>&1 | tee coverage.log
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    # Log summary
    COVERAGE=$(jq '.coverage_percent' tarpaulin-report.json)
    echo "coverage_summary: ${COVERAGE}%"
    echo "::set-output name=coverage::${COVERAGE}"
    
    # Fail if below threshold
    if (( $(echo "$COVERAGE < 70" | bc -l) )); then
      echo "::error::Coverage ${COVERAGE}% below minimum 70%"
      exit 1
    fi

- name: Upload to Codecov
  uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
  with:
    files: lcov.info
    fail_ci_if_error: true
    verbose: true
```

### Per-Module Coverage Logging
```rust
// In test harness, log coverage per module
#[cfg(test)]
mod coverage_logging {
    use std::process::Command;
    
    fn log_module_coverage(module: &str) {
        let output = Command::new("cargo")
            .args(["tarpaulin", "--out", "Json", "--packages", module])
            .output()
            .expect("tarpaulin failed");
        
        if output.status.success() {
            let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
            let coverage = json["coverage_percent"].as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
            println!("coverage_module: {}={:.1}%", module, coverage);
        }
    }
}
```

## Usage
```bash
# Generate coverage report
cargo tarpaulin --out Html

# View report
open coverage/tarpaulin-report.html

# Quick coverage check
cargo tarpaulin --out Stdout --skip-clean

# Module-specific coverage
cargo tarpaulin --packages beads_rust --out Html -- --test storage
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tarpaulin.toml configured with proper exclusions
- [ ] Coverage report generates locally
- [ ] CI workflow for coverage with threshold check
- [ ] Coverage badge in README
- [ ] Per-module breakdown logging
- [ ] Minimum 70% coverage enforced
- [ ] HTML + Lcov + JSON outputs generated

 {"id":"beads_rust-gxhx","title":"[P2] Fuzz: merge_issue harness lacks IssueValidator::validate() assertion","description":"Gap: fuzz/fuzz_targets/merge_issue.rs has a custom assert_issue_invariants() (line 334) that checks non-empty ID, non-empty title, priority 0-4, content hash consistency, unique labels/deps/comments — but does NOT call IssueValidator::validate() from src/validation/mod.rs:29. The validator checks ~7 additional rules the fuzz harness misses: ID format (is_valid_id_format), title max length (500 chars), description max size (100KB), timestamp ordering (created_at <= updated_at), estimated_minutes range, closed status invariants (closed_at required when status=Closed, forbidden when status!=Closed|Tombstone), and external_ref whitespace/length limits. The markdown_import fuzz target (line 34) already demonstrates the correct pattern: construct a candidate Issue, call IssueValidator::validate(), and fail if validation errors are returned. Target fuzz harness: merge_issue. Invariant to assert: every MergeResult::Keep(issue) and MergeResult::KeepWithNote(issue, _) should pass IssueValidator::validate() — or the harness should explicitly document which validation rules fuzz-generated inputs are expected to violate and filter those before asserting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_2","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:28.653891572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:43:51.607310439Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:43:51.606968249Z","close_reason":"merge_issue fuzz target now asserts IssueValidator::validate on all Keep/KeepWithNote outputs; normalize_issue fixed to produce valid IDs (lowercase prefix-hash), sanitized external_ref, and correct closed_at invariants; 2000 fuzz runs pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-gxr9","title":"E2E tests: comments command","description":"# E2E Tests for `comments` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br comments add <issue_id> <text>` - Add comment to issue
- `br comments list <issue_id>` - List comments on issue
- `br comments --json` - JSON output mode

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. Add single comment, verify in list
2. Add multiple comments, verify order (newest last)
3. List comments with --json, validate structure
4. Add comment to issue with existing comments

### Error Cases
5. Add comment to non-existent issue → error
6. Add empty comment → error or rejection
7. List comments on issue with no comments → empty list

### Edge Cases
8. Comment with special characters (quotes, newlines, unicode)
9. Very long comment (near limits)
10. Comment on closed issue (should work)

## Test Infrastructure
- Use tests/common/cli.rs BrWorkspace
- Capture stdout/stderr with logging
- Assert JSON shapes match expected
- Verify comment persists in DB and JSONL

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_comments.rs
- [ ] 10+ test functions covering above cases
- [ ] Tests use real storage, no mocks
- [ ] Rich tracing logs for debugging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperLake","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:25:40.126803216Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:13:12.960509970Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T16:13:12.960509970Z","close_reason":"E2E comments tests fully implemented: tests/e2e_comments.rs has 13 comprehensive tests covering comments command functionality.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-gxr9","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
